channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Niger And Mali’s Claims Of Terrorism: Growth Of Extremism In The Sahel As Threat To Global Security
item.description: Niger and Mali have accused terrorist groups of escalating violence in the Sahel region, posing a threat to global security. The claims come amid growing instability in the area, with extremist groups expanding their reach.

item.title: Trump cancels U.S. envoys’ visit to Pakistan for talks; says 'does not mean restart of war'
item.description: US envoys' talks with Pakistan were cancelled by Trump, who said it does not signal a restart of war. Iran's Foreign Minister left Islamabad without meeting the US delegation, and commercial flights resumed from Tehran's airport for the first time in two months.

item.title: Why locker room talk thrives in Indonesia’s digital spaces
item.description: Indonesia's universities are grappling with online harassment scandals, sparking debate over women's safety. Activists and policymakers are pushing for change in the country's digital spaces, where locker room talk has thrived.

item.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
item.description: Trump cancelled US envoys' talks with Pakistan over Iran, citing no plans for a direct meeting. Iran had earlier stated there were no plans for a meeting with the US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

item.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: Armed groups launched co-ordinated attacks across Mali, with clashes reported in the centre and north. Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north, in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

item.title: How serious is the rift in NATO?
item.description: NATO is divided over joining a potential Iran war. Trump is furious at Europeans for refusing to participate. Trump fury over Europeans' refusal to join Iran war. How serious is the rift in NATO?

item.title: Family longest held in US immigration detention re-arrested after release
item.description: The El Gamal family was re-arrested after being released from 10-month US immigration detention. Lawyers say El Gamal family detained by Trump administration hours after returning home from 10-month detention.

item.title: Trump claims progress in Mideast wars, but grievances could reignite them
item.description: Trump claimed progress in Mideast wars, citing reduced conflict, but grievances remain. Trump claims progress in Mideast wars, but grievances could reignite them AP News.

item.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: Militants and separatists launched coordinated attacks in Mali's capital and other cities, with JNIM claiming responsibility. The attacks, one of the largest in recent years, targeted several locations in Bamako and four other cities.

item.title: Six killed in Israeli strikes on south Lebanon: Health Ministry
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in south Lebanon killed six people, according to the Health Ministry. The strikes targeted projectiles launched from Lebanon, which were intercepted by Israel's military. Hezbollah condemned the strikes as a "blatant violation" of ceasefire understandings.

item.title: Gaza Civil Defence says Israeli strikes kill 13
item.description: Israeli strikes in Gaza killed 13 people, including two police officers, according to the territory's Interior Ministry. The Gaza Civil Defence reported the casualties. According to the territory’s Interior Ministry, two of those were police officers.

item.title: Trump puts onus on Iran’s authorities as they project hardened stance
item.description: US President Trump shifted the blame to Iran's authorities as they hardened their stance on the nuclear issue. Some officials said Tehran must not entertain further talks amid threats and a blockade.

item.title: Police arrest demonstrators at pro-Palestine rally in Berlin
item.description: Police arrested two women at a pro-Palestine demonstration in Berlin. Video footage shared on social media shows two women being arrested at a pro-Palestine demonstration in Berlin. Police arrest demonstrators at pro-Palestine rally in Berlin.

item.title: Trump says Kushner, Witkoff no longer going to Pakistan for Iran talks
item.description: US President Trump announced that Jared Kushner and Robert Witkoff are no longer going to Pakistan for Iran talks. The decision was made public through a live update on CBS News.

item.title: Israel says it will attack Hezbollah 'forcefully' as four killed in strikes on southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel vowed to attack Hezbollah 'forcefully' after four people were killed in strikes on southern Lebanon. The Israeli military carried out the strikes. Israel says it will attack Hezbollah 'forcefully' as four killed in strikes on southern Lebanon.

item.title: Japan’s New Care Workers: Bodybuilders, Wrestlers and M.M.A. Fighters
item.description: Japan is recruiting bodybuilders and athletes to work in nursing homes due to a staff shortage. Young athletes are being recruited to plug a staff shortage in Japan’s nursing homes.

item.title: Viktor Orbán steps back from Parliament after landslide loss, vows to rebuild Hungary’s ’national
item.description: Viktor Orbán stepped back from Hungary's parliament after a landslide loss, vowing to rebuild his party. His caucus will be "radically transformed" and he won't take his seat. Mr. Orbán said his party’s caucus in parliament would be “radically transformed” following the election loss, and that he would not take his seat.

item.title: Hungary's Orban will not sit in parliament after election loss
item.description: Viktor Orbán's party suffered an election loss, and he announced he won't sit in parliament. His party's parliamentary group will undergo significant changes. Hungary's Orban will not sit in parliament after election loss.

item.title: Arsenal narrowly beat Newcastle to regain Premier League lead over Man City
item.description: Arsenal beat Newcastle 1-0 to move three points clear of Manchester City. Nervy Arsenal beat Newcastle 1-0 in the EPL to move three points clear of Manchester City, who hold a game in hand.

item.title: China Hints Its 4th Aircraft Carrier Will Be Nuclear-Powered
item.description: China announced plans to build its fourth aircraft carrier, which will be powered by nuclear energy. This move marks a significant development in China's military capabilities, as it was the last major country to adopt aircraft carriers.
